results.title: Heavy Rainfall in Amravati
results.description: अमरावती शहरात मान्सूनच्या पहिल्याच जोरदार पावसामुळं अनेक शासकीय कार्यालय आणि घरांमध्ये पावसाचे पाणी शिरल्यानं गंभीर परिस्थिती निर्माण झाली आहे.

results.title: Delhi Health Scam: Medical equipment deals worth Rs 600 crore now under ED scanner
results.description: The ED has begun a money laundering probe into an alleged Rs 600–650 crore Delhi health procurement scam, seeking tender, payment and vendor records linked to medical equipment and hospital supplies.

results.title: US restrictions of advanced AI systems a "knee-jerk reaction.” says Jay Chaudhry, founder, Zscaler
results.description: Indian-origin Jay Chaudhry, founder, chairman and CEO of San Jose, California-headquartered company Zscaler said that concerns around restricted access to advanced AI models by Anthropic are overblown. In an interview with ET's Technology Editor, Surabhi Agarwal, he also criticised recent US restrictions around advanced AI systems calling it a 'knee-jerk reaction.” The company has a market capitalisation of roughly $20 billion and Zscaler was among the select 40 companies that received early access to Anthropic’s advanced AI model, Mythos, under Project Glasswing.

results.title: Maharashtra government's proposal to abolish Hazur Sahib Act, huge anger in Sikh community
results.description: There is intense anger in the Sikh community over the Maharashtra government's proposal to repeal the Hazur Sahib Act 1956 and implement a new law. Sikh leaders have accused the government of taking unilateral decisions without consultation, calling it interference in religious autonomy and traditions. The community has warned that religious rights will not be compromised.

results.title: Hyderabad scientists identify drug against pox viruses
results.description:
HYDERABAD: Scientists at the Indian Institute of Chemical Technology (IICT) have identified an existing anti-cancer drug that could potentially work against dangerous pox viruses, including the virus responsible for lumpy skin disease (LSD) in cattle and mpox (formerly monkeypox) in humans. The researchers used artificial intelligence (AI) and computational analysis to identify Lapatinib, an already [...]HYDERABAD: Scientists at the Indian Institute of Chemical Technology (IICT) have identified an existing anti-cancer drug that could potentially work against dangerous pox viruses, including the virus responsible for lumpy skin disease (LSD) in cattle and mpox (formerly monkeypox) in humans.

The researchers used artificial intelligence (AI) and computational analysis to identify Lapatinib, an already approved cancer drug, as a promising antiviral candidate. The study suggests that the drug may prevent the virus from replicating inside host cells by blocking a key viral protein. The findings are based on computer-based modelling and require laboratory validation before any clinical use.

Researchers screened FDA-approved medicines

The team analysed the structure of the LSGK protein using AI-based techniques and screened 88 US Food and Drug Administration (FDA)-approved kinase inhibitors to identify compounds capable of blocking the viral protein.

Among the drugs tested, Lapatinib showed the strongest potential to inhibit the target protein in the lumpy skin disease virus.

Study may aid research on other pox viruses

The researchers said the findings could also support the development of treatments for other pox viruses, including mpox and smallpox-related viruses. They added that the next phase of the research will involve laboratory experiments to confirm the drug's antiviral activity before any further development.

The study was carried out by Amanchi Srinivas, Racha Vaishnavi, Anmol Sinha, Apara Chengalvala, Julie Gupta, Nalla Saikumar, Bhamidipati Pranav, and Yedla Poornachandra. It has been published in the international journal Computers in Biology and Medicine.

]]>
